resources.string: OTP will be sent via SMS
Please enter valid name and phone number
Failed to send OTP. Please try again.
Create your first room from a photo!
Delete old rooms to create new ones
Long press to rename or delete
A room with this name already exists
You\'ve reached the limit of %d rooms. Delete some rooms to create new ones.
Are you sure you want to delete \'%s\'? This action cannot be undone.
Tap the heart icon on rooms to add them here
Are you sure you want to logout?
Quality affects rendering detail and performance. Higher quality may impact battery life.
Controls how fast the camera moves when using the joystick. Choose the speed that feels most comfortable for you.
Adjust the default dimensions for rooms created from single photos. These values will be used as starting points when detecting room boundaries.
Enable debug mode to see detailed detection information and performance metrics. Adjust thresholds to control detection sensitivity and filtering behavior.
Use window-tiled Part4b to reduce memory. Requires tiled .pte models on device.
Run the full SHARP pipeline (Part1–4b) in C++. Single Part4b only, no tiles. May reduce overhead.
Use batch-4 models for faster 1x patches. Disable if app crashes after "Part1+Part2 ready".
Limit splat count (0 = unlimited). 300k saves ~10s PLY write + faster viewer. 1.18M = full quality.
Default: 16 (4×4 grid, faster Part1+2). 25 = full 5×5 grid (best overlap, slower) with possible softness at patch boundaries when using 16.

GPU Part3/4 on Vulkan. Requires portable Part1+2 (e.g. sharp_split_part1_int8.pte + sharp_split_part2_int8.pte, or matching fp16/fp32 pairs) in the same folder as your Vulkan stack — room creation will not start without them. Put all .pte in files/models_cpuvulkan_hybrid/ for one-folder sideload. Legacy files/models_vulkan/ is migrated into this folder on launch, then removed when empty.
Show detection boxes and timing information
Shows Calibrate wall in the room menu and the Tap to calibrate pill when SmartyPants (brain) is active.
Shows the live camera feed behind furniture identifications before you start segmentation.
Phase 1 release: This app is currently offered for non-commercial use only. A future commercial release may be subject to different terms.
This app uses the following open source software.
Copyright (c) Ultralytics. Licensed under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.0. This Phase 1 release is non-commercial. For commercial use, see Ultralytics.
Copyright (c) SharpML contributors. Licensed under the MIT License.
Copyright (c) Google LLC. Licensed under the Apache License 2.0.

This product was developed with assistance from third-party AI and machine learning tools and platforms. We acknowledge their role in research, prototyping, engineering assistance, visual experimentation, and model workflows during development.
These acknowledgements are provided for transparency only. References to third-party companies, products, or models do not imply sponsorship, endorsement, or affiliation.
Apple frameworks, platforms, and technical references informed parts of our on-device 3D and machine learning development workflow, including work related to Sharp ML on Apple platforms.
OpenAI tools, including ChatGPT, were used to support product ideation, implementation assistance, drafting, and technical exploration during development.
Anthropic tools were used to support research, code assistance, reasoning through implementation details, and development iteration.
Luma AI tools and product references informed parts of our visual AI and 3D workflow exploration during product development.
Ultralytics tools, documentation, and the YOLO ecosystem supported our object detection and segmentation development workflows.
Are you sure you want to sign out?
For best quality, please visit our partner stores
Can\'t find what you\'re looking for?
Contact our support team and we\'ll get back to you as soon as possible.
support@paafekt.com has been copied to your clipboard.

Hi Paafekt Support Team,

I need help with:
Phones without AR depth support only get approximate measurements, especially for furniture.
How do I create a 3D room?
Tap the photo icon in the top-left corner of the home screen, then take or select a photo of your room. You\'ll see two options to choose from for creating your 3D room.
How do I take a photo to create a room?

Tap the camera icon on the home screen to take a photo directly. You can choose between Standard (1x) or Wide Angle (0.5x ultra-wide) lens. Hold your device steady and tap the capture button. The photo will then be used to create your 3D room.
What are the two room creation options?

When you select a photo, you can choose between: 1) AI-Powered 3D Room - automatically creates a realistic 3D room using artificial intelligence, or 2) Manual Setup - lets you draw the room boundaries yourself for more control.

This option uses smart technology to automatically turn your photo into a 3D room you can walk through. Just pick a photo and the app does the rest - no drawing or adjusting needed. It works like magic!

With Manual Setup, you draw the outline of your room\'s walls on the photo. This gives you more control over exactly how the 3D room looks. It\'s great when you want to fine-tune the room shape yourself.

Try AI-Powered first - it\'s faster and works great for most rooms. If the result doesn\'t look quite right, use Manual Setup to draw the walls exactly where you want them. You can always try both and see which one you prefer!
What kind of photos work best?
For best results, take photos in good lighting with the camera held level. Try to capture the entire room including floors, walls, and ceiling edges. Avoid blurry or dark photos.
Why is my room generation failing?
Room generation may fail if the photo is too dark, blurry, or doesn\'t show enough room features. Try taking a new photo with better lighting and a wider angle.
How many rooms can I create?
You can create up to 1000 rooms. Delete older rooms to make space for new ones. The room count and total storage used are shown at the top of your home screen.
How do I save a room?
After generating a 3D room, tap the save icon (download arrow) in the toolbar to save it with a custom name. Rooms that aren\'t saved will be deleted when you tap Back.
What does the brain icon do?
The brain icon starts SmartyPants identify mode. It uses the live camera to find furniture and objects, shows labels and boxes, and lets you select one or more labels before tapping Segment.
How do I take a screenshot?
When SmartyPants is active (brain icon is green), a share icon appears on the bottom-right. Tap it to save a screenshot of the view with AI detections to your Photos library.

Furniture segmentation now works from the live room viewer. Select detected labels first, then Segment creates a cut-out overlay only for those selected furniture items so you can judge fit in the 3D room.
How do I segment furniture from a photo?
In the room viewer, tap the brain icon, point the camera at the furniture, tap one or more detected labels to select them, then tap Segment. Tap Stop to return to live identifications.
Why isn\'t my furniture being detected?
Object detection works best with clear, well-lit environments where furniture is clearly visible. Make sure the object isn\'t partially hidden or too far from the camera.
How do I view my 3D room?
Tap on any room in your home screen to open the 3D viewer. Use touch gestures to rotate and zoom, or use the joystick at the bottom to move around inside the room.
How do I navigate inside the room?
Use the joystick at the bottom center of the screen to move around. Drag it in any direction to fly through the room. Use the recenter button (viewfinder icon) to reset the camera position.
What do the arrow buttons do?

The arrow buttons on the side of the 3D view move the camera: up and down change the view height; left and right pan the camera. You can also drag on the screen to orbit the room and use the recenter button (viewfinder icon) to reset the view.
What does the MB number mean?
The MB (megabytes) shown in rooms indicates the file size. This helps you manage storage. The total storage used by all rooms is shown at the top of the home screen.
How do I place furniture in my room?
After you tap Segment, the selected furniture appears as an overlay in the room. Drag to position it, pinch to resize, and rotate to adjust its orientation.
Can I use a sample room instead of my own?
Yes! The app provides sample rooms (Vintage Living Room, Cozy Living Room) for you to experiment with. Access them from the home screen to try out features without creating your own room first.
The 3D model provides a visual approximation of your room\'s layout. The dimensions shown are relative units from the AI model, not exact real-world measurements.
For USDZ rooms, go to Settings and look for Room Dimensions options. For AI-generated (PLY) rooms, the dimensions are determined by the AI model.
How is furniture overlay size estimated?

First tap the brain icon, select detected items, then tap Segment. Use the sizing control when you want room-relative scale. On phones with AR/depth support, the app uses live camera depth for a more realistic furniture size; without depth, furniture measurements are approximate.
What is the small measurement readout?

The Furni size pill appears only when sizing mode is on and the app has a usable live height estimate. Room measurements may also appear in the room viewer. On phones without AR, these readings are less reliable, especially for furniture.
The furniture mask looks wrongly zoomed—what can I do?
There is no overflow reset now. If the overlay looks off, turn the brain icon off and on again, or pinch the furniture manually to fine-tune its size.
What happens when I pinch the segmented furniture?

Pinching makes the furniture overlay larger or smaller. If sizing mode is on and the Furni pill is visible, that pill updates to the current resized furniture estimate for the room. If sizing mode is off, pinch only changes the overlay visually on screen.
Does taking a photo in the app help room quality?
Yes. On supported devices, choosing Take a photo (instead of picking from the gallery) uses the in-app camera with depth where available, which can improve how your 3D room is built and scaled.
Drag the circles to adjust boundaries
Select a photo to get started
How do you want to create your 3D room?
Tap an option below to continue
Successfully downloaded %s. View it in your models list.
Your 3D model has been saved successfully.
An error occurred while generating your 3D model.
Do not select screenshots - they may cause black patches
Capture or select a photo of your room
Use joystick to move • Pinch to zoom
Enter a name for your room
Your room has not been saved yet. If you go back now, you will lose this preview.
The app encountered an error. You can send a report to help us fix it.
Email is not configured on this device. You can copy the error details and send them manually to support@paafekt.com
Select camera lens for your room
Standard 1x lens • Hold in any orientation
Uses ultra-wide 0.5x lens for wider field of view
Hold steady • Ultra-wide lens captures more
Camera permission is required to take photos
Can I add multiple furniture pieces?
Yes! You can add multiple furniture pieces to your room. Simply segment each piece and add them one by one.
How do I check if furniture fits?
After adding furniture, you can resize and reposition it in your 3D room to see how it fits with your existing space and other furniture.

While SmartyPants is on in supported 3D room viewers, a Placement Intelligence card summarizes whether the detected object\'s size fits your room bounds, shows floor-fit hints and corner suggestions when available, and notes when depth is estimated. Tap the header to expand full details, or leave it collapsed to save space.
How does the app pick a color for aesthetic scoring?

The app averages the RGB color of visible pixels inside the AI furniture cutout (the same segmented overlay you see on camera). That mean color is compared to colors sampled from your room\'s floor, walls, and ceiling. If the cutout is not ready yet, a fallback color is taken from the room palette until segmentation supplies a real sample.
What do harmony, contrast, and style fit mean?

These are simple design heuristics, not a judgment of taste. Harmony describes how the furniture color relates to the room palette (for example analogous or complementary hues). Contrast is how different the brightness is from typical floor or wall colors. Style fit compares broad style labels inferred for the room with a default modern profile, so it is approximate.
When might aesthetic scores look low or show \"clash\"?

Bright, saturated objects often score worse when their hue does not line up with the app\'s harmonious color schemes against your room—for example acid green, magenta, purple, or safety-orange props against warm beige or tan rooms, or loud primaries in a room sampled as mostly one hue family. Very similar brightness to the floor or wall (brown-on-brown, gray-on-gray) can lower contrast even if you like the look. Rooms inferred as rustic or traditional can show lower style fit because the furniture profile uses default modern/minimal tags. Dull gray or muddy averages from busy backgrounds may land in a neutral band instead of clash. Treat scores as guides only and trust your eye for real decor.
Cannot save: room folder not found
Pinch with two fingers to zoom the room. When Furniture Fit is on, pinch also scales the segmentation overlay.
Tap the brain to start live furniture identification. Tap one or more detected items, then tap Segment to mask only those selections.
Tap the camera to save a picture of the room to Photos.
Drag furniture to move • Pinch to resize • Drag outside to pan room
No rooms yet\nTap the image icon to get started
⚠️ Do not use screenshots - use actual photos
How would you like to create your room?
This usually takes about 2 minutes. Hang tight — we\'re building something nice for you.
Tap %1$s to see progress and when your room is ready. Tap %2$s to set up room boundaries manually.\n\nDo you really want to go back?
Save room first to use AI detection
Your room textures have been extracted from the photo. Tap Save to keep this room.
Sign in with your phone number
By continuing, you agree to our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy
Move the device slowly to detect floors and walls.
Tracking paused — move device to resume.
Tap two points on a surface to measure.
Camera permission is required for AR measurement.
Wait until tracking is stable, then tap again.
No surface hit — aim at a floor or wall.
Place two points first (tap surface twice).
AR segment: %1$.3f m\nSHARP bbox: %2$.3f × %3$.3f × %4$.3f m\nChoose which axis this segment matches for isotropic scale.
That axis is invalid for scaling.
Calibration updated for this session only (room not saved to disk yet).
You can leave this screen. When the room is ready, it will appear in your Home list.
Tap AI Room to see progress. Generation keeps running if you leave; your room will appear on the Home list when it is ready.
Full room-fit sizing works best when metric furniture size is available. Color harmony below still applies.
Fits room bounds. No clear free-floor region yet.
Current furniture footprint exceeds the detected room extents.
Depth is estimated until catalog or semantic data is available.
